change: loop initialized
lesson: Every run: resurface one past lesson and re-check it before the gate.

change: seed
lesson: Body text contrast must be >=4.5:1 (WCAG AA). Light-gray-on-white is the most common failure.

change: seed
lesson: Icon-only buttons need an aria-label, or axe flags button-name (critical).

change: icon button emoji -> inline svg (aria-hidden)
lesson: Emoji inside a button SATISFIES axe's button-name rule. The gate goes green while a screen reader announces 'key'. Use an aria-hidden inline SVG for icons so a missing aria-label is actually catchable.

change: run axe at 375px and 1280px, not just default 1280x720
lesson: axe analyzes whatever viewport is current. Running it once before any setViewportSize only ever tested desktop. Mobile-only contrast/naming failures passed silently.

change: lift muted contrast to #5b6470; add aria-label to provider button
lesson: #b8b8b8 on white is 1.9:1. Nowhere near AA. Muted greys need ~#5b6470 minimum on a white card to clear 4.5:1.

change: capture viewport screenshots for the visual record
lesson: A design loop that logs only text cannot show you the design. Capture the viewport at each tested width; fullPage crops badly on centred layouts.

change: pixel-first focus check with disclosed computed-style fallback
lesson: Playwright waits for element STABILITY; a page with continuous motion (WebGL canvas) costs ~12s per element and animations:'disabled' only freezes CSS. Cap the timeout and fall back, but SAY which method ran.

change: split 'hover uses more than colour' into affordance + colour-only
lesson: A check phrased as 'is X done badly?' passes vacuously when X is absent entirely. Ask 'does X exist?' first, then 'is it done well?'

change: measurements briefed to the auditor but never marked settled
lesson: Telling a judge a measurement is settled removes the only independent check on the measurer. Brief it, invite challenge, and run blind periodically as the control.

change: extracted invariants from a published design spec instead of porting it
lesson: A spec's specifics are brand (#0066ff, Messina, J/K); only the reason behind them transfers. Port the invariant, discard the instance.

change: measure hierarchy on all four levers instead of judging it whole
lesson: Hierarchy = size + weight + contrast + spacing, and it is a RELATIONSHIP. Measure differences, not values. A closed set of sizes proves nothing: 12/13/14px is three values and one rank because the steps are under 1.1x.

change: workbench shell made durable: assert nav + panel exist AND the toggles change state; panel drag-to-resize
lesson: A control that renders but does nothing passes a PRESENCE check vacuously. A durable UI guard must exercise BEHAVIOUR (click it, assert state changed), not just query that the element exists. Same shape as CF-065: affordance present is not affordance working.

change: built the design doctrine store (contrarian+evidence gate, review-date anti-stale) under a full phased Trident run
lesson: A gate that PRINTS "FAIL" but EXITS 0 is not wired. The trigger must propagate the exit code (--strict), or CI stays green on input the gate already rejected. The cold audit caught this; presence of a check is not enforcement of it.

change: doctrine overlay inlined (no iframe), table redesigned: opinion=scope+claim bullets, vs renamed conventional wisdom, evidence collapsed to a count
lesson: A file:// iframe of a same-folder sibling DISPLAYS fine; Chrome only blocks cross-origin SCRIPTING, not rendering. So the fix for a picky embed is to navigate its src, not reach into it, or inline the content and skip the iframe entirely. The server was a preview-pane artifact, not a browser requirement.

change: stage embeds the prototype via iframe srcdoc (base64) instead of src to a sibling file
lesson: An iframe whose src points to a SEPARATE local file renders BLANK over file:// (Chrome gives each file:// doc an opaque origin and blocks nested local-document loads). srcdoc embeds the HTML inline, so it renders everywhere and is same-origin. Deliver state through the embedded page own contract (inject its ?state via replaceState), do not reach across the frame.

change: locked state drawn + workbench state-switching fixed (srcdoc drove no state; dropdown junk option removed)
lesson: Two bugs found ONLY by rendering the workbench and looking, not by a green script (CF-070). (1) srcdoc state-switch: history.replaceState fails on about:srcdoc, so expose the prototype show() globally and call it (same-origin). (2) template-literal escape trap: a regex like /<slash body>/ written inside a backtick template has its backslash eaten, corrupting the emitted script; use string .replace, not regex, for client JS built inside a template.

change: adopted: iterate web prototypes via a local server, not file://
lesson: Iterating via a local server (npm run serve / vite) is the norm and cheaper. file:// silently breaks sibling iframes, ES modules, and fetch (opaque origin); the workarounds cost more rework than the server (they produced CF-070 and a broken state-switcher). Start a server to iterate; keep file:// as a bonus, not the goal.

change: built loop-integrity + self-grade + rule-coverage checks; caught rule-coverage reporting a vacuous 32/32
lesson: A coverage checker that matches a rule to a checker by loose keyword (fallback: the rule first word) reports everything LINKED, because words like state/size/focus appear everywhere. That is a vacuous pass (CF-065) in the instrument built to measure coverage. Require an EXPLICIT rule->check mapping; under-claim rather than over-claim. Honest result: 11/32, not 32/32.

change: added a deterministic state-switch gate (drives the dropdown, asserts the stage switches) and a forward cutoff on coverage
lesson: CF-070 closed deterministically: verify-render now drives #st and asserts the embedded prototype switches, so a broken state-switch fails a check, not just the eye. Coverage made forward-only: historical runs predate the checker wiring and backfilling their teaching was rejected, so they are exempt and coverage is required from here on (gates apply forward).

change: completed the design treatment of all 4 state-matrix states (loading/error/success/locked); fixed locked (unreachable + colour-alone), a dead ink.resume() call, and loading-unreachable-via-?state
lesson: A cold audit is only cold if the GRADER MODEL differs from the maker. Two Opus subagent audits (fresh context, SAME model as the maker) both missed a dead ink.resume?.() that froze the canvas permanently after the first error, and a loading state the review path could not reach; a Sonnet audit caught both on the first pass. Context-decorrelation is not model-decorrelation. log-audit.mjs now records a required grader and check-selfgrade rejects grader==maker, so the loop can PROVE an audit was cold instead of trusting it.

change: kinetic-type reduced to ONE focal string with a directional masked slide (was a random scramble on three elements)
lesson: A motion hook is an ATTENTION mechanism, so spend it on ONE element (staging), never several — three moving things compete and dilute the focal point. Motion needs DIRECTION: a reading-order left-to-right reveal reads as fast and intentional; motion with no vector reads as noise. Every intermediate frame must be legible — a random-glyph scramble is decorative gibberish that encodes nothing true (design.md motivated-vs-decorative forbids it) unless "decoding" is literally the message. The masked slide (opacity stays 1) is also the contrast-safe technique: a fade drags the text through a low-contrast transient the a11y gate catches at load. Sources: Disney Staging (Thomas & Johnston, The Illusion of Life); Material motion (directional, informative); Val Head, Designing Interface Animation.

change: hardened teach-after-feedback from a memory reminder to a deterministic gate (teach-gate)
mechanism: Real teaching = mechanism + senior comparison + source. A fix stated without these is a changelog: the learner gets a patched file, not transferable vocabulary or a principle they can apply to the next problem. Naming the mechanism is what makes a one-off correction reusable.
senior: A design mentor never just fixes your work. They name the principle you missed, show how a staff designer would have caught it upfront, and point to the canon so the lesson survives your taste changing. Fixing without teaching keeps you dependent; teaching makes you independent.
source: House-rule 1 (deterministic > written reminder — a rule that only lives in memory decays); cognitive apprenticeship (Collins, Brown & Newman 1989 — experts must MODEL and ARTICULATE reasoning, not just complete the task).
lesson: Blunder: I taught only when reminded, and a memory is the weakest enforcement tier. Fix: teach-gate makes an untaught lesson FAIL npm test. Every feedback lesson from here carries mechanism/senior/source or the gate blocks it.
